compiled_state.title: As a developer, implementation-plan and the decision-maker iterate on proposals
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement the resolution loop between implementation-plan and the decision-maker agent — accept, revise, or escalate to user after 2 cycles.
**Why**: Allows implementation-plan to defend its proposals or incorporate the decision-maker's counter-proposal, with bounded iteration to prevent analysis paralysis.
**Scope**:
- IN: Accept/revise/escalate logic, cycle count persistence to /tmp state file, user escalation after 2 cycles, integration with implementation-plan flow
- OUT: Agent definition (Story 2), proposal generation (Story 1)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, implementation-plan can accept the decision-maker's selection or revise proposals and resubmit
  <- Satisfies: "implementation-plan may accept the recommendation or revise its proposals and resubmit"
- When this story is complete, the resolution loop is bounded to 2 cycles with user escalation after
  <- Satisfies: "up to 2 revision cycles before user escalation"
- When this story is complete, cycle count is persisted to /tmp/approach-resolution-<story-id>.json (survives context compaction)
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Cycle count must be persisted to /tmp state file, not held in LLM context — context compaction resets counters
- [Reliability] Must set timeout: 600000 on agent re-dispatch
- [Maintainability] State file should follow naming convention: /tmp/approach-resolution-<story-id>.json; define TTL and cleanup consistent with preplanning-context pattern
